stars.description: Original Apollo 11 Guidance Computer (AGC) source code for the command and lunar modules.
stars.highlights: This repository contains the original Apollo 11 Guidance Computer source code, providing authentic historical documentation of the software that landed humans on the moon. It offers a rare look at 1960s-era assembly programming for mission-critical aerospace systems.
stars.worthReading: It's a fascinating piece of computing history that demonstrates how complex real-time systems were engineered with extreme constraints, offering insights into foundational software engineering practices.
stars.scoreReason: Low relevance to frontier AI topics despite moderate freshness from recent starring, with high concreteness as specific historical artifact.

stars.description: Self-healing browser harness that enables LLMs to complete any task.
stars.highlights: A self-healing browser harness that enables LLMs to autonomously complete web-based tasks with built-in error recovery. It provides robust automation capabilities for AI agents interacting with dynamic web environments.
stars.worthReading: It addresses a critical pain point in agent workflows by making browser interactions more reliable and resilient to common web automation failures.
stars.scoreReason: Good freshness with moderate traction, high relevance to agent tooling, and concrete implementation of self-healing browser automation.

stars.highlights: Vega provides a declarative visualization grammar for creating interactive graphics through JSON specifications. It enables data-driven visualizations that work across multiple rendering targets (SVG, Canvas) with a consistent API.
stars.worthReading: It's a foundational visualization framework used in many data science and AI workflows, offering a structured approach to creating complex visualizations programmatically.
stars.scoreReason: Moderate relevance for AI tooling and good concreteness, but limited freshness as an established visualization library rather than cutting-edge AI development.
